Polygonum hydropiper, commonly known as water pepper, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Polygonaceae family. It has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Polygonum hydropiper include flavonoids, alkaloids, and polyphenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Polygonum hydropiper include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues.
